summary: Coach Watch-derived Hero Badge replacement - All tests passed. PulseIndicator now correctly shows CW-derived labels (Hot Opportunity, No Signals, Low Priority, Active) on Journey pages instead of legacy pulse labels (Neutral/Warm/Hot/Cold). Backward compatibility verified on PipelinePage which still uses legacy pulse prop.
critical_code_review_comments: PulseIndicator.js correctly implements dual-mode: coachWatchState prop (new CW-derived) takes priority over pulse prop (legacy)
CW_STATE_BADGE mapping covers all 10 Coach Watch states with correct labels and colors
Animated dot correctly restricted to active states: hot_opportunity, active_conversation, emerging_interest, re_engaged
JourneyPage.js line 432 correctly passes coachWatch?.state to PulseIndicator
PipelinePage.js line 360 maintains backward compatibility with legacy pulse prop

success_rate.backend: 100% (Coach Watch API returns all correct fields)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Hero badge replacement complete. PulseIndicator component now supports both coachWatchState (CW-derived) and pulse (legacy) props. Journey pages use CW-derived labels; PipelinePage uses legacy labels. Key data-testid: pulse-label, pulse-indicator, cw-headline.

frontend_playwright_tests.hero_cw_consistency: ✅ Stanford hero badge matches CW card headline
frontend_playwright_tests.pipeline_page: ✅ Loads with kanban board, no errors
